model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: St. John's and Kansas are virtually identical in adjusted efficiency — KenPom #20 (+24.80 AdjEM) vs. #21 (+24.48), Torvik #18 vs. #22 — making this a near-coin-flip on paper, but the injury context decisively tips the balance toward the Red Storm. Kansas is playing without senior guard Kevin McCullar Jr., who was ruled out for the entire NCAA Tournament with a bone bruise and was one of the Jayhawks' two leading scorers, while Hunter Dickinson is playing through a dislocated shoulder that caused him to miss the Big 12 Tournament entirely. St. John's, the Big East Tournament champions at 27-6, arrive fully healthy with Rick Pitino's elite defensive system (12th nationally in AdjDE) and Big East battle-testing that matches Kansas's Big 12 credentials. The marginal efficiency edge, full health, and the significant personnel loss for Kansas make St. John's the right side in what is otherwise one of the most evenly matched games in the bracket.

reasoningStep.title: St. John's vs. Kansas — Round of 32 Efficiency, Injury and Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Both teams are statistically near-identical by every major rating system, but Kansas's confirmed loss of Kevin McCullar Jr. and Hunter Dickinson's shoulder injury create a meaningful health disadvantage that tips this razor-thin matchup to St. John's.
evidence: St. John's KenPom #20 AdjEM +24.80, AdjOE 119.9 ranked 46th, AdjDE 95.1 ranked 12th nationally; Kansas KenPom #21 AdjEM +24.48, AdjOE 118.4 ranked 57th, AdjDE 94.0 ranked 10th — St. John's holds a slight offensive edge while Kansas has a marginally better defense.

Kevin McCullar Jr. officially ruled out for the entire 2026 NCAA Tournament with a bone bruise on his knee; he missed six of Kansas's final 12 regular-season games and the entire Big 12 Tournament, removing one of the Jayhawks' two leading scorers.

Hunter Dickinson dislocated his right shoulder and missed the Big 12 Tournament; he reported his shoulder feels good and is expected to play but is not at full health and carries meaningful injury risk in a physical Round of 32 matchup.

St. John's enters as Big East Tournament champions at 27-6 under Rick Pitino with no significant injury concerns reported, an elite defense ranked 12th nationally, and Big East battle-testing that matches Kansas's Big 12 credentials.
Torvik ranks St. John's #18 vs. Kansas #22, consistent with KenPom, confirming a slight but consistent edge for the Red Storm across all major rating systems.

Edit Profile My Subscriptions { document.cookie = `${cookieName}=; domain=${domain}; path=/; max-age=-1000`; }); window.location.href = this.href;"> Sign Out 54°F News Sports Business WSU Restaurants Games Obituaries Finance Best of Wichita Home Customer Service About Us Archives Cancel Subscription Contact Us Customer Service Subscribe Manage Print Subscription Advertise Contact McClatchy Advertising Stay Connected Mobile Apps & eReaders Newsletters Social Media Facebook Twitter Instagram YouTube Read today's Edition Best of Wichita Local Events News Crime & Courts Local Databases Nation & World Politics Elections Weather News Sports Wichita State Varsity Kansas Chiefs K-State Kansas Outdoors Royals FIFA World Cup Business Business Personal Finance Carrie Rengers Entertainment & Food Dining with Denise Horoscopes Puzzles & Games All Entertainment Opinion Opinion Guest Commentary Submit a Letter Columnists Dion Lefler Obituaries View Obituaries Place an Obituary Video Featured Reality Check Uniquely Best Of Wichita Shopping/Reviews Product Reviews Health and Wellness Software and Business Shopping Deals & Offers Amazon Prime Press Releases Sponsored Content Paid Content by BrandPoint Sponsored Articles Family Features Classifieds Place a Classified Ad Post a Job Browse Classifieds Place an Ad - Celebrations Search Jobs Search Legal Notices Advertising Place an Ad Staffing Solutions Political | Advocacy Advertising University of Kansas Will KU Jayhawks’ McCullar, Dickinson play in NCAA tourney? Latest injury updates By Gary Bedore Updated March 19, 2024 6:37 PM Lawrence UPDATE: Kevin McCullar was ruled out for the NCAA Tournament on Tuesday. Get the details here . Kansas Jayhawks forward Hunter Dickinson, who suffered a dislocated right shoulder in the second half of KU’s regular-season finale against Houston and then missed a second-round Big 12 tournament loss to Cincinnati, has been making steady progress in his recovery, coach Bill Self said Sunday. ‘“Hunt’ is doing great,” Self said of the senior Dickinson. “He actually practiced the last two days non-contact. “We’ve done a lot of dry (non-contact) stuff. That way he could be out there. He’ll go contact tomorrow (Monday).” Self has said Dickinson and senior guard Kevin McCullar should be available for the NCAA Tournament. The Jayhawks open the Tournament with a first-round matchup against Samford on Thursday night in Salt Lake City. “Kevin … I have not seen doing anything,” Self said. “The entire game plan on him was hopefully Monday he can be good to go. If he’s not good enough to go tomorrow he won’t (practice) and we’ll hope (for) Tuesday. I don’t know as much on Kevin as I do ‘Hunt.’” 2024 NCAA Men's Bracket by The Kansas City Star on Scribd Self brought up Dickinson Sunday when asked if the Jayhawks had been shooting the 3-pointer with more confidence at pract

Got it Edition CA NY Open College Basketball navigation Sports Sports Sports Plus Sports Betting Columnists Olympics NFL MLB NBA NHL WNBA NWSL PWHL NCAAB NCAAF Sports Entertainment Golf Tennis Wrestling MMA Soccer NCAAB St. John’s Scores Stats Standings Teams Players Odds Injuries Search Search College Basketball Meet St. John’s: Roster, player to watch, key numbers entering March Madness By Joseph Staszewski Published March 15, 2026, 7:36 p.m. ET Meet the Big East champion St. John’s Red Storm, who enter March Madness as the No. 5 seed in the East. Location: Queens Enrollment: 16,651 undergraduates (20,435 total) Access the St. John's beat like never before Join Post Sports+ for exciting subscriber-only features, including real-time texting with Zach Braziller about the inside buzz on St. John’s. TRY IT NOW Coach: Rick Pitino (3rd season) Last NCAA appearance: 2025 NCAA Tournament history: 31 appearances, 28-33 How they got here: St. John’s started slowly and was 9-5 after an ugly home loss to Providence on Jan. 3. Since then, few teams have been better. The Johnnies have won 19 of their past 20 games and became the first Big East team ever to win back-to-back outright regular-season and postseason titles. The versatile frontcourt of Zuby Ejiofor, Dillon Mitchell and Bryce Hopkins has been dominant, and Dylan Darling has emerged as a key piece at point guard. The nation’s top-ranked transfer class delivered two more titles to Queens. Starters G Dylan Darling 7.0 ppg, 2.5 rpg, 2.5 apg G Oziyah Sellers 10.7 ppg, 2.9 rpg, 1.7 apg St. John’s Red Storm Forward Zuby Ejiofor and Guard Dylan Darling watching the NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament Selection Show. Corey Sipkin for the NY POST F Dillon Mitchell 8.2 ppg, 7.0 rpg, 3.1 apg F Bryce Hopkins 13.5 ppg, 6.2 rpg, 2.0 apg F Zuby Ejiofor 16.3 ppg, 7.1 rpg, 3.5 apg Explore More Jamal Mashburn reveals how Rick Pitino guided St. John’s back to the national conversation: ‘Masterful’ Jamal Mashburn bullish on Zuby Ejiofor’s NBA outlook

Kansas Jayhawks forward Hunter Dickinson, who suffered a dislocated right shoulder in the second half of KU’s regular-season finale against Houston and then missed a second-round Big 12 tournament loss to Cincinnati, has been making steady progress in his recovery, coach Bill Self said Sunday. ‘“Hunt’ is doing great,” Self said of the senior Dickinson. “He actually practiced the last two days non-contact. “We’ve done a lot of dry (non-contact) stuff. That way he could be out there. He’ll go contact tomorrow (Monday).” Self has said Dickinson and senior guard Kevin McCullar should be available for the NCAA Tournament. The Jayhawks open the Tournament with a first-round matchup against Samford on Thursday night in Salt Lake City. “Kevin … I have not seen doing anything,” Self said. “The entire game plan on him was hopefully Monday he can be good to go. If he’s not good enough to go tomorrow he won’t (practice) and we’ll hope (for) Tuesday. I don’t know as much on Kevin as I do ‘Hunt.’” 2024 NCAA Men's Bracket by The Kansas City Star on Scribd Self brought up Dickinson Sunday when asked if the Jayhawks had been shooting the 3-pointer with more confidence at pract

Location: Queens Enrollment: 16,651 undergraduates (20,435 total) Access the St. John's beat like never before Join Post Sports+ for exciting subscriber-only features, including real-time texting with Zach Braziller about the inside buzz on St. John’s. TRY IT NOW Coach: Rick Pitino (3rd season) Last NCAA appearance: 2025 NCAA Tournament history: 31 appearances, 28-33 How they got here: St. John’s started slowly and was 9-5 after an ugly home loss to Providence on Jan. 3. Since then, few teams have been better. The Johnnies have won 19 of their past 20 games and became the first Big East team ever to win back-to-back outright regular-season and postseason titles. The versatile frontcourt of Zuby Ejiofor, Dillon Mitchell and Bryce Hopkins has been dominant, and Dylan Darling has emerged as a key piece at point guard. The nation’s top-ranked transfer class delivered two more titles to Queens. Starters G Dylan Darling 7.0 ppg, 2.5 rpg, 2.5 apg G Oziyah Sellers 10.7 ppg, 2.9 rpg, 1.7 apg St. John’s Red Storm Forward Zuby Ejiofor and Guard Dylan Darling watching the NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament Selection Show. modelTrace.content: {"pick":{"gameId":"r32-east-2","winnerId":"st-john-s","confidence":0.62,"rationale":"St. John's and Kansas are virtually identical in adjusted efficiency — KenPom #20 (+24.80 AdjEM) vs. #21 (+24.48), Torvik #18 vs. #22 — making this a near-coin-flip on paper, but the injury context decisively tips the balance toward the Red Storm. Kansas is playing without senior guard Kevin McCullar Jr., who was ruled out for the entire NCAA Tournament with a bone bruise and was one of the Jayhawks' two leading scorers, while Hunter Dickinson is playing through a dislocated shoulder that caused him to miss the Big 12 Tournament entirely. St. John's, the Big East Tournament champions at 27-6, arrive fully healthy with Rick Pitino's elite defensive system (12th nationally in AdjDE) and Big East battle-testing that matches Kansas's Big 12 credentials.
